characters.name: Ben
characters.description: The titular "Baby Daddy," a bartender who unexpectedly becomes a father to a baby girl. Big Brother Worship: Though it's been more or less phased out after the drama of the love triangle, Ben very much did look up to and idolized Danny, and still has a slight dependence upon him. Bumbling Dad: Especially in the beginning, the entire premise of the show isManchildBen learning how to become a single dad to Emma. By the end of the series, he's no longer bumbling. The Casanova: Though downplayed due to the love triangle, Ben is nevertheless shown to be a constant womanizer. After all, that's how he got Emma. Embarrassing Nickname: Riley called him "Ben-Wetter" in the first episode, but it hasn't shown up again since.

characters.name: Tucker
characters.description: Ben's best friend and roommate. Berserk Button: Don't takehisfood from the fridge. Camp Straight: Tucker has a high-pitched voice, is obsessed with cleanliness and grooming, and displays some effeminate mannerisms. He's also a skirt-chasingCasanova Wannabe. Honourary Uncle: To Emma. He even calls himself her "fruncle".notePortmanteauof "friend" and "uncle" The Napoleon: He compensates for his shortness with a bold personality.

characters.name: Danny
characters.description: Ben's older brother and Emma's paternal uncle. Beware the Nice Ones: He's mostly a sweet-hearted lug who a little bit of aMomma's Boy, but if you hurt someone he cares about (especially Riley,) youwillregret it. Big Brother Instinct: When one of Riley's boyfriends punched Ben, Danny immediately stepped in:Danny:Big mistake. [punches him in the face] Embarrassing Tattoo: He plans to get his girlfriend Amy's name tattooed on his wrist to show his devotion to her, but because he's so heavily drunk, he ends up withRiley'sname. He eventually does get the "Amy" tattoo on his other wrist... onlyhoursbefore Amy dumps him. Lovable Jock: He's a pro-hockey player who clearly loves and prioritizes his friends and family over the visages of wealth.

characters.name: Bonnie
characters.description: Ben and Danny's mother. Amicable Exes: After learning the real reason her husband left her (Ray is gay), the two seemingly patch up their relationship and become civil parents to their adult sons and grandparents to Emma. Doting Parent: To Danny, but not so much with Ben. She's also become a doting grandmother to Emma. Former Teen Rebel: She was a bit of a wild child when she was young as seen when Riley finds out about Bonnie's first wedding in Vegas when she was eighteen. Hidden Depths: For all her apparent shallowness, she had the hardest time when she got pregnant with Danny as a teenager. She got kicked out of her house at 17, had to move in with her boyfriend's family, was verbally abused and stranged from her mother for keeping her baby and still did everything she could to keep her sons safe and happy.

characters.name: Riley
characters.description: Ben's close friend and Danny's best friend since they were little. Berserk Button: Donotcall Riley"Fat Pants"or she will put you in a headlock and slam your head into hockey glass. Beware the Nice Ones: When she got fired from her job at a law firm for being "too nice" and not having the killer instinct a lawyer needs, she (thanks to aWounded Gazelle Gambitfrom Bonnie) threatened to sue the firm for Bonnie's (pretend) injuries, and threatened to make every single dirty trick the firm ever did a matter of public record. She ended the whole thing with a big smile and saying "Who's too nice now?" She got her job back. Butt-Monkey: On more than one occasion, the insanity of Ben and Co. has spilled over to cause mayhem in her life. Childhood Friends: With the Wheeler brothers.

characters.name: Emma
characters.description: Out of Focus: While she of course remains a constant in Ben's life and his character development, the show has moved away from the group raising her to the wacky antics they all get themselves into. Platonic Co-Parenting: Her primary caregivers are her father, uncle, and their roommate/herHonorary Uncle.

characters.name: Angela
characters.description: Ben's ex-girlfriend and Emma's mother, an aspiring actor. It's All About Me: Tries to be a mother to Emma, but ultimately chooses her acting career. Before leaving, she tries to sabotage Ben and Riley's budding relationship despite Ben having feelings for Riley. Her final appearance has her briefly re-enter Emma's life simply to help her image by portraying herself in an interview as a single mother who raised Emma all by herself. Ms. Fanservice: Very attractive, getting several scenes in tank tops, towels, and a bathrobe. Small Role, Big Impact: Doesn't appear until the end of Season 2 and is only in 5 of the show's 100 episodes, but is the one that gave birth to Emma and dropped her off at Ben's doorstep, kickstarting the events of the show. Unseen No More: After only being mentioned throughout the first season, she finally appears in person in the Season 2 finale.

characters.name: Mary Hart
characters.description: A fictionalized version of the TV personality.
